mechanism_narrative: The 3-month change in FEDFUNDS is evaluated every month; a rising policy rate increases discount rates and tightens credit conditions, producing lower S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or Rank IC is above -0.03 in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in HOUST signals contraction in housing-linked demand and future profits for cyclicals, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 6-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST contractions are followed by nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE identifies weakening labor income and recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases produce positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if negative-return hit rate is below 55% over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The 3-month trend in INDPRO is used continuously; rising industrial production indicates stronger real output and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO trends are not followed by higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative trends, or if rolling Rank IC is below 0.03 for 3 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS is also rising, markets price a restrictive Fed reaction and margin pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation crosses above 4% and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-and-tightening trigger has positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if its return distribution is not worse than unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A short-term acceleration in RSAFS signals stronger consumer spending and near-term revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered RSAFS accelerations have nonpositive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if positive-return hit rate is below 52% after transaction-date alignment.

mechanism_narrative: Weak PAYEMS growth combined with a rising UNRATE signals a broad labor-market turn, lowering income growth and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average gain falls below 100 thousand and UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS and UNRATE trigger is followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if it fails to underperform unconditional returns by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth alongside rising RSAFS indicates broad real-demand strength that supports earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds 2% and RSAFS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 and RSAFS expansion trigger does not produce higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than all other observations, or if rolling Rank IC is below 0.03.
